heading: Enable Customer-Managed Keys for a Project
body:
You must enable customer key management for a project before you can
enable it on a cluster in that project.

Enter the key version resource ID for the Google Cloud KMS key
that you want to use to encrypt your data at rest.

Your key version resource ID is the fully-qualified resource name
for a [CryptoKeyVersion](https://cloud.google.com/kms/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.keyRings.cryptoKeys.cryptoKeyVersions#CryptoKeyVersion).

> **Important:** You must enter a valid ID before you can configure your
> authentication method.

Atlas supports two authentication methods for GCP KMS:

- **Service Account (Recommended)**: Use an Atlas-managed
  service account specific to your project to authenticate to GCP.

- **Static Credentials**: Provide a customer-managed service account key.

Service Account

First-time Setup

If you have not yet set up a service account for your Atlas project:

- Click Authorize a new service account.
  The Encrypt Data with GCP KMS dialog box appears.

- Click the Set Up button to create your GCP resources.
  Atlas provisions the GCP [folder](https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/creating-managing-folders)
  and creates the service account. The folder is required in order to
  create service accounts.

- Select the Atlas service account from the dropdown menu
  or [create a new one](create-org-api-key).

- Run the [gcloud](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install) commands
  that appear to grant the service account the following
  required permissions:

  - Enable Encrypt/Decrypt operations

    ```sh
    gcloud kms keys add-iam-policy-binding \
      <key-name> \
        --location <location> \
        --keyring <keyring-name> \
        --member <ATLAS_OWNED_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL> \
        --role="roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyEncrypterDecrypter"
    ```

  - Enable GetPublicKey operations

    ```sh
    gcloud kms keys add-iam-policy-binding \
      <key-name> \
        --location <location> \
        --keyring <keyring-name> \
        --member <ATLAS_OWNED_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL> \
        --role="roles/cloudkms.viewer"
    ```

- Click Validate & Finish to confirm.

Existing Service Account

If you have an existing service account for your Atlas project:

- Click Authorize a new service account.
  The Encrypt Data with GCP KMS dialog box appears.

- Select your existing
  Atlas service account from the dropdown menu
  or [create a new one](create-org-api-key).

- Run the [gcloud](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install) commands
  that appear to grant the service account the following
  required permissions:

  - Enable Encrypt/Decrypt operations

    ```sh
    gcloud kms keys add-iam-policy-binding \
      <key-name> \
        --location <location> \
        --keyring <keyring-name> \
        --member <ATLAS_OWNED_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL> \
        --role="roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyEncrypterDecrypter"
    ```

  - Enable GetPublicKey operations

    ```sh
    gcloud kms keys add-iam-policy-binding \
      <key-name> \
        --location <location> \
        --keyring <keyring-name> \
        --member <ATLAS_OWNED_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL> \
        --role="roles/cloudkms.viewer"
    ```

- Click Validate & Finish to confirm.

Static Credentials
